mnemonics: FANBOYS for AND, OR, NOT: Forget 'And, Nor, But, Or, Yet, So' is for English conjunctions, but use it to remember the basic logic connectives are its own set: AND (conjunction), OR (disjunction), NOT (negation).
keyPoints: A proposition is a statement that is either true or false, but not both.
The truth value of a compound proposition defined by a connective is determined solely by the truth values of its components.
The implication (p → q) is false only when p is true and q is false; it is true in all other cases, which is often counter-intuitive.
commonMistakes: Confusing the converse (q → p) or the inverse (¬p → ¬q) with the original implication (p → q); they are not logically equivalent.
Assuming that the disjunction 'OR' is always exclusive (XOR); in logic, unless specified, 'OR' is inclusive and is true when both components are true.
